context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements a 13-week cash flow model with the right inventory purchase timing logic, treats sales tax as a scheduled outflow, builds a one-time payment calendar, uses known transactions for near-term weeks, and keeps capital expenditures separate from operating cash flows.

checklist.name: 13-week / 91-day window
checklist.description: Forecast covers exactly 13 weeks (91 days forward), not a different horizon such as 12 weeks or 6 months

checklist.name: Inventory lead time
checklist.description: Inventory purchase order receipt date is computed as PO date + lead_time_days (45 days), not as payment date directly

checklist.name: Inventory payment after receipt
checklist.description: Inventory payment date is receipt date + payment_terms_days (30 days), not directly from PO date

checklist.name: Target weeks of stock = 8
checklist.description: The inventory reorder logic targets 8 weeks of forward stock coverage (target_weeks_of_stock = 8)

checklist.name: Sales tax as scheduled outflow
checklist.description: Sales tax remittance ($22,000) is modeled as a scheduled cash outflow in the outflow schedule, NOT deducted from inflows or treated as a revenue reduction

checklist.name: One-time payment calendar
checklist.description: Software renewal ($28,000 at week 6) and trade show expense ($15,000 at week 9) are included as discrete one-time outflow entries in specific weeks

checklist.name: Near-term known transactions
checklist.description: Weeks 1–4 use the specified known transaction amounts (confirmed PO payment week 2 = $95,000, confirmed customer payments week 3 = $67,000) rather than model-derived estimates for those entries

checklist.name: Running cash balance
checklist.description: Running cash balance is computed as opening_cash + cumulative sum of net weekly cash flows (not recalculated from scratch each week)

checklist.name: Outflow categorization
checklist.description: Output table separates outflows into at least two categories (e.g. operating/recurring vs one-time, or operating vs inventory vs one-time)

checklist.name: CapEx separation
checklist.description: Capital expenditures are kept in a separate section or variable from operating cash flows — NOT mixed into the operating outflow total

checklist.name: Opening balance carried forward
checklist.description: The $450,000 opening balance is used as the starting point for the running cash balance calculation
